primary_purpose: Org-wide GitHub configuration repository for the AFI Protocol organization, holding the org profile/repository map plus a README describing intended reusable GitHub Actions workflows and templates (none of which are actually present in the repo yet).
classification_rationale: Contains only README.md, package.json (private, name '.github', description 'Organization-wide GitHub configuration for AFI Protocol'), and profile/README.md (the org repository map). No schemas, contracts, type defs, code, or specs. It is GitHub org tooling/config plus a documentation index, i.e. SUPPORTING infrastructure for the org, not a normative protocol artifact, reference implementation, or research.
protocol_touchpoints: replay/determinism (named only: afi-reactor replay, afi-artifacts replay)

reference_only_assumptions.quote: afi-reactor – Signal DAG / orchestrator, Codex integration, replay.
afi-token – Canonical token contract logic for AFI (Epoch Pulse, rewards, wiring).
This org is structured around how AFI actually works in the wild: the Machine, the Mint, the Ops, and the Lore.

contradictions.title: Repo map labels afi-reactor as THE orchestrator (no reference-vs-normative distinction)
contradictions.detail: The org repository map presents afi-reactor as 'Signal DAG / orchestrator' as a singular org component, with no caveat that the DAG framework is pluggable per the portable-protocol surface. This is a low-severity reinforcement of the reactor-as-the-orchestrator tension, but it is only a one-line repo index entry, not a normative claim.
contradictions.evidence: profile/README.md:12 '- **afi-reactor** – Signal DAG / orchestrator, Codex integration, replay.'

contradictions.title: Stale/archived repo names persist in the org map
contradictions.detail: The map lists archived repos afi-agents and afi-construct, matching the known stale-naming tension. They are correctly flagged as archived/historical here, so this is mostly informational, but the map is a discovery surface that may mislead newcomers.
contradictions.evidence: profile/README.md:73-74 '- **afi-agents** – Early CLI entry-points and manifests. / - **afi-construct** – Early simulation dojo before the multi-repo layout.'

contradictions.title: Root README advertises reusable workflows/templates that do not exist in the repo
contradictions.detail: README.md claims the repo contains workflows/, ISSUE_TEMPLATE/, PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md, CONTRIBUTING.md, CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md, SECURITY.md and five reusable workflows (validate-typescript.yml etc.), but the only files present are README.md, package.json, and profile/README.md. The documented org-wide validation/security workflows are not actually provided.
contradictions.evidence: README.md:21-25 lists 'validate-typescript.yml ... validate-solidity.yml ... security-scan.yml'; find shows only ./README.md ./package.json ./profile/README.md (no .github/workflows).

recommended_action: tag stale / fix in follow-up: add the missing reusable workflows and templates the README advertises (or trim the README to match reality), and add an org-map disclaimer that DAG/orchestrator/store choices are reference-not-mandatory per the portable-protocol surface.

notable_findings.title: Repo contains no schemas, contracts, type defs, or replay artifacts
notable_findings.recommendation: Treat as SUPPORTING org config; exclude from normative/replay synthesis.

notable_findings.title: README documents org-wide validation/security workflows that are absent
notable_findings.evidence: README.md:19-25 'Reusable Workflows - validate-typescript.yml ... validate-solidity.yml - Solidity contract validation ... security-scan.yml'; no workflows/ dir exists
notable_findings.recommendation: Either implement the reusable workflows referenced by other repos via uses: AFI-Protocol/.github/.github/workflows/...@main, or update README to remove unimplemented claims.

notable_findings.title: Pattern hits (DAG/orchestrator, canonical, mint, vault, replay, codex, registries) appear only as repo-map labels in profile/README.md
notable_findings.evidence: profile/README.md:9 'DAG reactor'; :12 'Signal DAG / orchestrator, Codex integration, replay'; :26 'Canonical token contract logic ... (Epoch Pulse...)'; :42 'vault utilities'; :62 'validator/mentor registries'
notable_findings.recommendation: No action; these are descriptive index entries, not normative definitions. Source-of-truth lives in afi-config/afi-token/afi-docs.

notable_findings.title: afi-protocol named as holder of 'Formal protocol spec, ADRs, versioned definitions'
notable_findings.evidence: profile/README.md:24 '- **afi-protocol** – Formal protocol spec, ADRs, versioned definitions.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Use as a pointer when locating the normative spec home; verify against afi-protocol and afi-config/schemas during synthesis.

notable_findings.title: Archived repos afi-agents and afi-construct still listed (stale-naming tension)
notable_findings.evidence: profile/README.md:69-76 'Archived (Historical Only) ... afi-agents ... afi-construct ... everything else is fossils.'
notable_findings.recommendation: Keep flagged as archived; acceptable since map explicitly marks them historical.
